{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,

    "R1.6": 0.5,

    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": ["R1.2", "R1.3", "R1.6", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.2", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The excerpt makes strong causal and comparative claims (e.g., wav2vec 2.0 'substantially improves calibration', token-level abstention 'recovers the tradeoff at fivefold lower abstention rate') whose internal logical coherence could plausibly be the review target.",
    "R1.3": "Multiple precise empirical figures appear (38% WER reduction, 12% refusal rate, 22% decoding cost reduction, 41% relative WER, 2.4% abstention rate); verifying these against stated experimental conditions is a natural reading of 'take a look'.",
    "R1.6": "Prose-level feedback is the most colloquial interpretation of 'take a look at this paragraph'; sentence structure, hedging language, and paragraph flow are all candidates for comment.",
    "R1.7": "'Vaibhava and Hermansky (2018)' is an unusual author-name format (single given name) and the cited title and reported results are unverifiable without access; citation accuracy is a plausible load-bearing concern under a looser interpretation of the delegation."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Are you primarily asking for feedback on writing quality and style, or on the scientific accuracy of the claims, evidence, and citations?"
}

rationale.R1.2: The excerpt makes strong causal and comparative claims (e.g., wav2vec 2.0 'substantially improves calibration', token-level abstention 'recovers the tradeoff at fivefold lower abstention rate') whose internal logical coherence could plausibly be the review target.
rationale.R1.3: Multiple precise empirical figures appear (38% WER reduction, 12% refusal rate, 22% decoding cost reduction, 41% relative WER, 2.4% abstention rate); verifying these against stated experimental conditions is a natural reading of 'take a look'.
rationale.R1.6: Prose-level feedback is the most colloquial interpretation of 'take a look at this paragraph'; sentence structure, hedging language, and paragraph flow are all candidates for comment.
rationale.R1.7: 'Vaibhava and Hermansky (2018)' is an unusual author-name format (single given name) and the cited title and reported results are unverifiable without access; citation accuracy is a plausible load-bearing concern under a looser interpretation of the delegation.

parsed.clarification_question: Are you primarily asking for feedback on writing quality and style, or on the scientific accuracy of the claims, evidence, and citations?
